body_txt: Along with stating that This Is a Competition , participants in competitive reality shows also like to declare that their current goal is not the formation of friendships. This makes sense in principle — whether you're trying to win a cash prize or the love of a D-list celebrity, it's not a good idea to be all buddy-buddy with your competition instead of focusing on your own performance — but in practice tends to mark the speaker as an arrogant douchebag who's probably going to lose. Like any reality show cliché, the producers do feed this line sometimes. If one actually holds this attitude, it can constitute Reality Show Genre Blindness in a Voted Off the Island -style show, as you are directly (fellow contestants) or indirectly (audience) lowering the opinions of the people who could send you home. Also, even many shows with skill-based eliminations and a single winner involve team-based tests ( The Apprentice , Drag Race ) or at least pay lip service to the virtues of friendship ( The Bachelor /ette ), so saying this phrase also implies one thinks they can win the contest all by themself... and you know what society thinks of those kinds of folks. It's ultimately a redundant and self-destructive phrase since everyone on the show is there to win; you gain nothing by going out of your way to say so, and it makes you look like a jerk or a threat. Characters who say this line are also prone to Cat Fights . Bonus points if the person who says it makes it into a Pun on the show's name (e.g. "This is not America's Next Top Best Friend !", "This is Flavor of Love , not Flavor of Friendship !", "This is not RuPaul's Best Friend Race !") This trope may be an applied form of the GIFT —as has been written by Reality TV columnists, many people who enter games with this attitude do not actually act like this in real life. They decide that within this game, the end justifies the means, and pause caring about what others think about them. As described below, this attitude is not exclusive to reality game shows; it's just the origin of the trope. In video games, "Stop Having Fun" Guys and Scrubs can embody this trope. Be very careful adding Real Life examples. See This Is a Competition for another stock reality show line. Related to Serious Business . Contrast I Just Want to Have Friends .
